text:
An American traveler is in disbelief after being along in a Chinese national park 🇨🇳 

This video is from Zhangjiajie national park one of the most beautiful places in the entire country. Not only is the traveler in disbelief by the trail on the side of the mountain but also how few people are actually there 

This is a common sight in China, even though the country has over 1 billion people, many tourist attractions feel a bit empty. In my opinion this is because of how large the country is and how many place there are to see. Of course the time of the year also plays a huge role #fyp #tellme #traveltiktok #china #zhangjiajie
author: @christian.grossi

text:
Wanna book a doggy plane with me? 👉👈🥺 prices for 14 and 18 seat non-stop chartered flight from Seattle to Amsterdam in August 2026, one way

Amsterdam has fewer breed restrictions for bully’s and DAFT visas 

We’re actually going to Spain or Portugal after we land in Amsterdam. 

Prices for 30-50 seat airliners are likely even cheaper, but I need to know what level of interest we’re working with before I can get firm numbers for those larger planes. 

Also workshopping a pet friendly hotel block in Tacoma (cheaper and less crowded than Seattle) with some doggy tours and excursions to get them ready for the long flight. Happy to help with any local recommendations you need! 

Get in! We’re going to Europe!! 

***Please do your own research regarding breed restrictions and visa eligibility!! I’m in no way a pro at this
author: @mariahbyourbroker

text: Replying to @evp When it comes to international travel, baggage limits can be a little confusing because they vary by airline, ticket class, and even your destination. Some airlines go by weight, while others go by number of bags and the rules can be very different depending on where you’re flying. The key is to always check your airline’s specific baggage policy before booking so you know exactly what’s included, what’s extra, and how to plan ahead. It can save you money, stress, and a lot of repacking at the airport. #internationaltravel #movingabroadtips #airporttips #travelingabroad #leavingamerica
author: @artfully.amberrae

Looking to leave the U.S. but not sure where to start?
Here are 5 of the easiest countries for Americans to move to-places with clear residency paths, affordable living, and a better quality of life.
From Portugal's Golden Visa to Mexico's Temporary Residency, these countries make starting fresh simpler than you think.
Your dream life abroad is closer than you realize.
